title: Partner/beneficiary outreach + indicator shortlist (TOPIC-CATALOG.md)
context: No partner or beneficiary is currently secured. The project's definition of 'shipped' requires a named beneficiary to accept and use the output — producing a brief without a recipient is not a success. Simultaneously, the candidate topic backlog (TOPIC-CATALOG.md) needs to be seeded with FAOSTAT indicators that are structural, low-controversy, and clearly suitable before any per-topic brief tasks are created. Both workstreams unblock the M0 pilot and must run in parallel.
objective: Open at least one partner-outreach thread with a candidate beneficiary channel and create the initial TOPIC-CATALOG.md shortlist of pre-screened FAOSTAT indicator candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one partner-outreach thread opened with a candidate beneficiary channel (educator, newsroom, civil-society group, library, or humanitarian analyst), documented with contact record and response status.

TOPIC-CATALOG.md created with at least 5 candidate FAOSTAT indicator entries, each pre-screened for structural/long-run nature, low controversy, and initial licence/sensitivity suitability (flagged if any concern).
Each catalog entry records: indicator code, domain, definition summary, reference period type, why it is structural/long-run, and any sensitivity or licence flag.
The pilot indicator for pilot-008 is identified from the catalog (e.g. food supply kcal/capita/day or cereal production trends) with rationale for its low-controversy selection.
Document committed to the repo; commit is DCO signed-off.
output: A committed TOPIC-CATALOG.md with an initial indicator shortlist and at least one documented partner-outreach thread.
